description_long: This is a vintage-style corded telephone with a metallic blue finish and gold accents, featuring a classic rotary dial on the front. The handset rests on a cradle at the top, connected to the base by a visible white cord. The rotary dial is circular with numbered finger holes, and the base is rectangular with a slightly sloped front panel. The handset is robust, with rounded earpiece and mouthpiece sections, and the cord appears to be made of flexible plastic or rubber. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century telephones, with a sturdy, utilitarian build. The surface shows some texture and wear, suggesting it is made of painted metal or heavy plastic.
description: A vintage blue corded telephone with a rotary dial, gold accents, and a visible white cord connecting the handset to the base. The handset rests on a cradle atop a sturdy, textured base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, gold accent plates, and the handset resting on the cradle.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the thickness of the handset and the sloped front panel with the rotary dial.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handset and the base, with the cord trailing to the side.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the handset profile and the rotary dial's edge, with the cord extending from the back.
